name: TableColumn
description: Typed column primitive for Table. Wraps a TableHeaderCell (auto-derived header per type) and one TableCell per row, choosing the type-appropriate variant. 12 column types — 1:1 with the Figma component set symbols.

props.name: type
props.description: Column type — determines header layout and cell rendering. 1:1 with the 12 type-* symbols in the Figma frame.

props.name: header
props.description: Column header text. Defaults to a per-type label (Text / Number / Date / Badge / User / Users / Two-line / Icon / Card / Actions). The checkbox type renders a select-all Checkbox in the header cell instead of text.

props.name: rows
props.description: Row data — one entry per cell. Shape varies by type: text/number/date use { text }; badge uses { badgeLabel, badgeIntent }; user / two-line+avatar use { text, initials | avatarSrc }; users-stacked uses { users: [{ name, initials?, src? }] (or legacy stackedCount); two-line uses { text, secondaryText }; checkbox uses { checked }; card uses { cardLast4 }; icon-shape / actions accept the generic { render } escape hatch.

props.name: sortable
props.description: Whether the header is sortable. Drives TableHeaderCell.sortable — direction is owned by the parent Table.

states.rationale: TableColumn is a layout container — it delegates all interactive state to composed cells (TableHeaderCell sortable/aria-sort, TableCell row hover via the parent Table). The root has no hover/pressed/focus of its own.

keyboard.Tab: Focuses interactive children in row order (Checkbox cells, action Buttons, sortable column headers)

accessibility.focusManagement: Root is a plain <div> — semantic table grouping (role="table" / role="grid", row primitives) is owned by the parent Table. Composed TableHeaderCell carries role="columnheader" + aria-sort; each TableCell carries role="cell". Action Buttons in actions cells are native <button>s with aria-label. Checkbox columns use native <input type="checkbox"> in both header (select-all) and each cell.

propertyMapping.note: 12 Figma type-* symbols map 1:1 to TableColumnType enum values.
Default per-type label (Text / Number / Date / Badge / User / Users / Two-line / Icon / Card / Actions). Checkbox column shows a select-all Checkbox in the header cell.
TableColumnRow shape varies by type — see props.rows.description.

useWhen: Composing a Table from typed column primitives
Need a consistent column-type vocabulary across many tables in the app
Demo / Storybook context for showing column-type patterns
doNotUseFor: Single cells in a custom row → use TableCell directly
Full table chrome (toolbar + filter row + body + footer) → compose Table + TableTopBar + Filters + columns

knownDeviations.description: The card type's masked digits use a small inline composition (•••• + last 4) rather than a dedicated DLS primitive. The Mastercard logo comes from the brand-logos asset module. Future enhancement: extract a <CardNumber> helper if used in more than ~3 places.

TableColumn intentionally does not own pinned / locked flags — this is a design decision, not a TODO. Rationale: (1) locked has zero render implication on the column itself; it's a UI-state flag the management surfaces (DropdownColumns hides the Pin button and skips drag targets; DropdownColumnActions hides Pin/Move/Hide rows) consume to gate user actions. Adding it to TableColumn would be a no-op prop. (2) pinned has a real visual implication (position: sticky on cells) but requires Table-level coordination — each pinned column's left offset depends on the cumulative width of pinned columns before it, which TableColumn alone can't compute. A correct implementation requires lifting a ColumnModel into the parent Table (columns: ColumnModel[] + onColumnsChange) where the model owns pinned/locked/hidden/order. Today the consumer derives the rendered TableColumn props from their own column state (typically driven by DropdownColumns + DropdownColumnActions). This works correctly until a real consumer needs horizontally-scrolling tables with sticky pinned columns, at which point the proper fix is the ColumnModel refactor, not adding props piecemeal to TableColumn.
